name: Eraring Big Battery
notable: Largest battery in the Southern Hemisphere. Co-located at the Eraring coal power station site. 700 MW across 4 stages: Stages 1 & 3 (460 MW, ~4h) operating; Stages 2 & 4 (240 MW, grid-forming, ~5.8h) under construction. Average 4.5h duration at full buildout.

eis_specs.document_title: Eraring Big Battery — State Significant Development Assessment (NSW IPC SSD-2022-0119)
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Eraring 330 kV substation (TransGrid), Lake Macquarie NSW (on-site, former power station)
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: On-site — located within the Eraring Power Station compound on Lake Macquarie, NSW. Former 2,880 MW coal plant 330 kV substation reused.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: IMPORTANT NOTE: This is a separate battery from the existing eraring-battery (150 MW Tesla Megapack). The Eraring Big Battery (700 MW Wartsila Quantum3) is a different, larger project also at the Eraring site. Both batteries use the same TransGrid 330 kV Eraring substation, eliminating major connection capex.
eis_specs.notes: 700 MW / 1,390 MWh (2-hour) BESS at Eraring Power Station, Lake Macquarie NSW. Developer: Origin Energy. Technology: Wartsila Quantum3 (LFP, CATL). EPC: Enerven. NSW IPC SSD-2022-0119. Now operating. Distinct from the 150 MW eraring-battery (Tesla Megapack). Both batteries exploit the exceptional TransGrid 330 kV infrastructure at this former 2,880 MW coal plant.

sources.title: Wartsila - Fourth stage of Eraring BESS
PV Magazine - Australia's biggest battery starts commercial operations
IPP Journal - Eraring BESS commercial operations
Batteries International - Origin ramps up to 700MW

stages.name: Stages 1 & 3
stages.notes: Stage 1 (460 MW / 1,070 MWh, 2h) plus Stage 3 (+700 MWh extension to ~4h). Commercial operations commenced Dec 2025. Built by Wartsila/Enerven.

stages.name: Stages 2 & 4
stages.notes: Stage 2 (240 MW / 1,030 MWh, 4h, grid-forming) plus Stage 4 (+360 MWh extension to ~5.8h). Target COD Q1 2027. AUD 80M committed for Stage 4.

timeline_events.title: Origin announces large battery at Eraring coal site
timeline_events.detail: Battery to be co-located at the Eraring coal power station site, initially 460 MW

timeline_events.title: Wartsila selected as technology supplier for Stage 1
Wartsila selected for Stage 2 (240 MW / 1,030 MWh)
timeline_events.detail: Stage 2 adds 240 MW of grid-forming battery capacity

timeline_events.title: Wartsila selected for Stage 3 (700 MWh storage extension)
Stages 1 & 3 achieve commercial operations
timeline_events.detail: 460 MW / 1,770 MWh (~4h duration) commenced commercial operations. Completed on time and under budget.

sources.title: PV Magazine - Australia's biggest battery starts commercial operations

timeline_events.title: Stage 4 construction commenced
timeline_events.detail: 360 MWh storage extension for Stage 2, AUD 80M investment. Target completion early 2027.

sources.title: Wartsila - Fourth stage of Eraring BESS
